surface_contract.explicit_runtime_value: ordinary explicitly constructed or injected service, Actor, or shared-state value

selects: enum case where that namespace owns the item
one exact statically named type-side extension item
one exact associated type in type context
one exact associated value in expression context
one exact associated function before call checking
explicit context or provider value with visible authority
never_selects: type token converted to a value
Trait evidence converted to a storable value

required_properties: no Resource, drop, finalizer, or unload responsibility

class_scope_static_preview_fence.candidate_owner_minimum: future final non-generic concrete ordinary class only
deferred_owners: data class with synthesized construction routes

rules.title: Capability decomposition replaces a companion object
rules.contract: Factories and constants use nominal type-side members, generic static behavior uses Trait associated requirements, extensions remain named, and runtime state has an explicit ordinary owner; no companion object or singleton is created.

rules.title: The four lookup domains are disjoint
rules.contract: Nominal type-side, named extension, Trait-qualified associated static, and explicit runtime value lookup never enter one another as fallback or ranking candidates.

rules.title: Generic associated static selection is explicit
rules.contract: A generic associated type, value, or function is selected only by <T as Trait>::item; T::item never searches visible Traits even when exactly one candidate appears visible.

rules.title: Syntactic context fixes associated item kind
rules.contract: Type context requires an associated type, expression context requires a value or function, and a call suffix requires a function; kind mismatch is terminal and never reinterpreted through another lookup domain.

rules.title: Associated type static selection composes explicitly
rules.contract: <T as Trait>::Assoc::member first selects one associated type and then performs nominal type-side selection on that normalized type without Trait or extension fallback.

rules.title: Static evidence identity survives lowering
rules.contract: TraitId, RequirementId, ConformanceId, TraitWitnessId, ImplementationId, SubstitutionId, and ResponsibilityId remain exact HIR, MIR, and API residue.

rules.title: Selection is compile-time and order independent
rules.contract: Source, import, use, and link order, expected result, implicit conversion, runtime lookup, provider search, and fallback neither create nor rank an associated static candidate.

rules.title: Owner-local factories keep bounded private authority
rules.contract: Only a def:: lexically declared in the nominal owner may receive that owner's private construction privilege.

rules.title: External capability code gains no private authority
rules.contract: Top-level qualified type-side functions, named extensions, and external conformances use ordinary visibility and cannot manufacture an owner-local private construction route.

rules.title: Associated values are immutable static material
rules.contract: The initial Stable let:: profile is const-evaluable or static-materializable, deeply immutable or Shareable, pure, safely publishable, acyclic, authority-free, borrow-safe, and free of Resource, drop, finalizer, and unload responsibility.

rules.title: Stateful capabilities require explicit values
rules.contract: A stateful factory, provider, registry, connection, allocator, or service is an explicit ordinary value, Actor, shared-state owner, or context value rather than hidden Trait or type-token state.

rules.title: Type tokens and witnesses are not implicit runtime values
rules.contract: A type name and a static conformance witness do not convert to a storable companion, metatype, factory, or provider value.

rules.title: Trait selection causes no activation
rules.contract: Associated requirement selection and witness materialization trigger neither function static activation nor any proposed class static activation.

rules.title: Class scope static remains fenced
rules.contract: Class scope#static has zero current source acceptance and remains PREVIEW_NONACTIVATABLE; removed static class source remains rejected.

rules.title: Function and class activation identities remain distinct
rules.contract: Stable function scope#static is owned by FunctionStaticOwnerId and is not a type field cache or class-wide barrier; a future type owner must use a separate identity and authority.

rules.title: No commit event is invented
rules.contract: This contract introduces zero CALL_INPUT_COMMIT events and refers only to the existing atomic ownership_commit where a future activation ordering needs a call boundary.

rules.title: Conformance implementations do not become nominal members
rules.contract: A def:: in a conformance implements one exact associated function requirement and does not add an ordinary Type::member or extension member.

rules.title: Existing P-level and product boundaries remain invariant
rules.contract: Semantic P0 remains zero, the exact existing feature P1 set remains 22 OPEN, this contract closes or creates no P1, and all 15 product lanes remain NOT_RUN.
